title: The Winnowing of the Threshing-Floor
subtitle: The Son of Man comes in majesty to separate the sheep from the goats

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
One Gospel can suffice you for salvation, said Augustine —
for here are held the merits and the demerits, the rewards and the torments, of the good and of the wicked.
Sit before this single page, and let it be enough.
gospel: When the Lord comes with his angels to judge,
all the nations shall be gathered together.
When the Son of Man shall come in his majesty,
and all the angels with him,
then shall he sit upon the seat of his majesty,
and all nations shall be gathered before him.
And he shall separate them one from another,
as the shepherd separateth the sheep from the goats;
and he shall set the sheep on his right hand,
but the goats on his left.
Then shall the King say to them that shall be on his right hand:
Come, ye blessed of my Father,
possess you the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
behold: Behold the Son of Man — the Son of the Virgin — coming to judgement in his majesty.
In his first coming he appeared to be judged, in a form able to suffer, weak.
Now he appears to judge, in the glorious form of a man.
He comes in human form, that he may be seen by all;
for if he came in the form of God, the wicked could not behold him.
In his train come all his angels with him — for the Judge's honour,
as witnesses of the deeds of men, and executors of the sentence he shall pass.
He sits upon the seat of his majesty, and shows forth his omnipotence.
Then are gathered before him all the nations — of every time, every condition, every age —
for no one shall then be able to lie hidden, nor to conceal himself.
By day the sheep and the goats are together in the pastures;
but in the evening the shepherd separates them.
Mark well the positions of the right hand and the left:
from that moment each part shall know which mercy, and which misery, hangs over it.
consider: In the sheep is understood the innocence of the good — their simplicity, their meekness, their fruitfulness.
In the goats is understood the perversity of the wicked — their foulness, their violence, their barrenness.
The sheep are the just: they wound no one, and when wounded by others, they endure.
The goats are sinners, for in them dwell by nature lust above all beasts, pride, quarrel, envy, gluttony, and clamorous chatter above all.
He shall separate them by merit, discerning all merits; by place, assigning the varied places; by name, calling these goats, those sheep.
He sets the just at his right, because they never knew the left;
the wicked at his left, because they never wished to know the right.
At the right are confirmed in eternal blessedness those who loved the eternal;
at the left, in eternal misery, those who desired the things of the present.
Now he is not called Son of Man, but King — to whom it belongs to rule his subjects, to make laws, to punish the transgressor, and to reward the keeper of the law.
Then Pilate shall not ask: Art thou a king then?
Then the Jews shall not say: We have no king but Caesar.
Mark here the most sweet invitation of the Lord, when he says: Come, ye blessed of my Father.
Before you were born, he says, these things were prepared and fitted for you, because I saw that you would be such.
O how happy and blessed are they who shall deserve to hear this sweet call directed to them,
and, invited by the Lord, to receive his kingdom.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, judge of the living and the dead,
in the last judgement deign to set me at thy right hand,
and then make me to hear that most sweet voice of thine,
which thou hast promised to thine elect, saying:
Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom;
and grant that I may not fear that evil hearing thou hast threatened to the reprobate,
saying: Depart, ye cursed, into everlasting fire.
O only-begotten Son of God, have mercy on us,
that we may never undergo that incurable pain —
to fall away from the glory of the heavenly kingdom, and to be burned in hell.

latinJewel.latin: De die sunt simul in pascuis oves et hædi, sed in vespere pastor eos segregat.
latinJewel.english: By day the sheep and the goats are together in the pastures, but in the evening the shepherd separates them.
